steps: Go to https://cloudinary.com and log in or create a free account
From the Cloudinary Console, click your product environment name in the top bar
Click 'Settings' (gear icon) in the left sidebar
Select 'API Keys' from the settings menu
Your Cloud Name, API Key, and API Secret are displayed on this page
Click the copy icon next to the API Key and API Secret to copy each value
Note your Cloud Name — it is required as part of the API URL path

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: API Key
credential_fields.description: Your Cloudinary API Key (used as the Basic auth username together with api_secret)

credential_fields.name: api_secret
credential_fields.label: API Secret
credential_fields.description: Your Cloudinary API Secret (used as the Basic auth password)

credential_fields.name: cloud_name
credential_fields.label: Cloud Name
credential_fields.description: Your Cloudinary cloud name (appears in all media URLs)

notes: Cloudinary uses HTTP Basic auth: api_key as username, api_secret as password
The cloud_name is part of the API URL path (e.g., /v1_1/{cloud_name}/image/upload)
Free tier includes 25 GB storage, 25 GB bandwidth, and 25,000 transformations per month
You can generate multiple API key/secret pairs in the console for different applications
Never expose the API Secret in client-side code — use upload presets for unsigned uploads

test_endpoint.description: Get account usage statistics (replace {cloud_name} with your cloud name)
